text.title: Flying to Peru with absolutely no plan | A Testament to Spontaneity
text.u: it's not that we have a short life to
live but that we waste a lot of it what
would you do if you had ultimate Freedom
the chance to do anything I could
conform to the path made for me or
choose to carve my
own every person has two lives and the
second begins after realizing you have
just one 79 years is what they say the
average lifespan is that's 79 Summers
and 79 Winters make some crazy choices
this is a time for taking risks for
doing things spontaneously and most
importantly doing more of what makes you
feel
alive welcome back to 105 kfm radio
today we're here talking with with
little Timothy what's up y'all it's
little Timothy you heard it here first
Peru is the best spot you can travel to
right now y all right there you have it
little Timothy has
[Applause]
spoken and just like that I convinced
some buddies and we bought tickets to
Peru 2
[Music]
the feel you all in all the guys landed
in Peru just fine and met up at a hole
in thewall Hostel in the middle of
theity all just excited to be back
together we played countless games of
pool and charged all of our gear for the
next coming
[Music]
days oh you know what I want to make a
video with what
song in we really wanted to Surf so we
got a taxi over to a beach that we
thought would have waves come to find
out the waves were dangerous and so was
the area we were in we were exploring
this abandoned building of some sort on
the beach when a whole entire Squad of
police officers pulled up on dirt bikes
raiding the building yelling at us in
Spanish we had no idea what was going on
moral of the story is we were in not
such a safe area and we needed to leave
no surfing was done this day
oh my
gosh oh my gosh yo after getting our
first taste of the country we took a
4-Hour bus ride into a deserted Oasis to
a place called
hakina this is in my opinion an
adventure capital of the world it's the
kind of place where you come for a
weekend and end up for a week surrounded
by mountains of sand it's a city that
feels disconnected from the
world hundreds of dune buggies line the
Mounds and take each however that was
not the thing that attracted us
here we out in the middle of nowhere
look at look where we are
at we are lit lit
indeed while exploring the town and
buying some ponchos we found a dune
buggy to drive us deep into the desert
strapped up with boots and Boards so
that's exactly what we
[Music]
did this is what it's all about we came
out here with very few expectations and
it's experiences like these that blow me
away all day long we rode up and down
down the dunes with a driver that didn't
seem to think twice about liability
there's some places on this planet that
just don't quite feel real almost like a
Moodie set and this is one of them boys
about to hit the Sands just took a hit
almost died in this just weeks ago we
were all finding out about this place
for the first time and now we were
having the times of Our Lives a lot of
times in life the best moments come
unexpectedly and this is a perfect
example one of my favorite travels
yet yes
[Music]
yes good bro I bet that's not the only
record for the video yeah some some got
decapitated here so uh maybe maybe look
at
[Music]
this
today we're going sand ding slash we
just finished we just finished it yes
sir we finished the night off by
watching the sunset on top of the cars
at the Bluffs and called it a night from
there let's do it let's go home and take
some showers eat some wings
boys you know I recently saw this video
from theovon and it's funny because he's
someone you can rarely ever take
seriously but he said something that
stuck with me he said nothing changes if
nothing changes and it was in one of
those core core videos that you see late
at night but it really meant something
to me and it's just a few words but it's
deep nothing changes if nothing changes
everybody has goals everybody has
aspirations and I think the norm is to
assume that things are just going to
fall into place that goal is just going
to be there with the only Factor being
time but that's just not the case if you
want to achieve your dreams achieve your
goals it takes action you need to make
the change you need to do the things
that are going to get you to your end
goal self-limiting beliefs are
everything and you have to truly believe
and not only believe but use logical
thinking you need to make a plan to get
to where you want to be in life what are
the things that you need to do to
progress and then it just comes down to
doing
[Music]
it been doing now we all bought oneway
tickets which means we didn't know how
long we would be there and more
importantly
where we would spend the most time at
and that came to be the city of Cusco
this place is one of my favorites it's a
Peruvian City with heavy Spanish
influence and it is it's alive that's
the only way to describe
it every night here was something new
and no matter where we went we always
found ourselves on the road back to
Cusco all in all this place has grown to
be one of my favorite cities in the
world plus it's the base camp for one of
the country's most prided
[Music]
landmarks from Cusco we hopped on a
train that took us through the Andes
mountain range for 3 hours dropping us
off in a town called AAS
cendes but getting to Machu Pichu from
here wasn't as easy as it may have
seemed once we arrived off the train we
noticed people started sprinting towards
the ticket office and so we started
sprinting too to find the biggest line I
have seen for anything ever and it took
about 10 to 12 hours maybe and we didn't
even get tickets we had to come back the
next day this is unbelievable
honestly in the meantime I explored the
city and got some local food I got lost
but this usually ends up leading to some
of the coolest stories a man named Juan
screamed out of his building to come
over and share beer with him so you know
I
had
graas God bless you Gracias my
friend later interactions like that are
exactly why I travel just coming to new
places with little to no plan and seeing
where the road takes you is probably one
of my favorite things to do in life and
I'd recommend it to almost anybody it
forces you to experience things you
couldn't imagine and talk to people with
stories you wouldn't believe anyways day
turned to night and the morning finally
came in which we would see Machu
picu this is a seriously
incredible so quiet we're like some of
the first people up here
here this right here is the exact moment
that ited hit me getting here felt so
accomplishing I'm 20 years old now and
ever since I was a child I was
fascinated by machui my dad watched The
History Channel and when I saw it on the
television I never thought that I would
be standing where I am now the boy I
once was gazing at the TV screen was now
face to face with it life is a wild game
but when you find the part of yourself
that truly lives instead of fears you'll
find yourself in a world of opportunity
you know life doesn't have to be some
big rat race you don't need to be the
richest man on the block with the
shiniest car and the most expensive toys
the truth is you can live a very
outgoing and adventurous life with very
few things you just have to know how to
use your legs and they'll get you
far standing here this place is
everything I at imagined it to be for
about 1 and 1/2 hours it felt like we
had the place to ourselves the land was
quiet and I had some time to sit and
think about how far I've
come we did some shooting and next thing
you know the entire city looked like an
amusement park it really isn't my type
of atmosphere but for something so
genuinely spectacular it can only be
expected we wrapped up at Machu Pichu
and headed home to good old Cusco this
whole trip we've been playing so much
chess and on the trade ride home a young
man sat down and beat our entire friend
they can't speak to each other so
they're speaking through this kid was on
top of the world I tell you we
eventually made it home and hung around
for a couple days before heading off on
our final mission in the
[Music]
country
con all right y'all so we are on our way
up to one of the highest peaks the
Peruvian Andes and the road here beside
me is the only way there and it's closed
for another two and a half
hours it's very hot and we need to make
some light of the
situation so we're going to play bosi
ball with
rocks what started as a 2 and 1 half
hour delay quickly turned into four so
we found ourselves messing around in
this dirt lot playing bot ball with
rocks and running chests on our travel
siiz board we even got our driver in on
it which was pretty fun looking back on
it this was a great memory even though
in the moment I never would have thought
about it that way it was minuscule and
honestly it was really hot it was hot as
[ __ ] eventually though the road cleared
up and we were back on the
road leading us miles into the remote
Andes driving on a ran out dirt road
over tht Cliffs barely wide enough for
two
cars this was pretty nerve-wracking but
it was one of the coolest drives I've
ever been on he dropped us off as close
as he could take us and we would have to
walk the rest of the way getting our
first reveal at the Village we would
call home for the next couple
days we finally were here we arrived
just in time for dinner and right as we
got there we realized this was more than
just your average hostel it's the only
building in town other than the farm so
everyone eats breakfast and also dinner
like a family we drank cocoa tea and ate
spaghetti introducing ourselves to all
the trekers and Backpackers from all
over the world the electricity was solar
powered and there was only one Outlet in
the whole hostel and the temperatures
dropped below freezing every night so
they supplied us with about 15 blankets
to keep us
warm these types of things may not be
for everyone but I know All The
Travelers this is exactly what you look
for in a travel experience all of us
enjoyed disconnecting and we got some
rest because we were here for one reason
and one reason
[Music]
only
[Music]
this was definitely the highlight of my
trip surrounded by 20,000 ft Peaks every
direction you looked in Was A new
scenery we explored for days seeing the
ins in outs of this remote Village and
hiked up a mountain to an Alpine Lake
that we had entirely to ourselves not a
single person but
us this was the most Freedom I've ever
possibly had we were really out here
doing it this is what life is all about
to me exploration and joyous times with
friends and
family the days passed quickly here and
our trip was starting to come to an end
we headed back to Cusco to talk about
what we may do
[Music]
next we finally wrapped it up in Peru as
a whole and decided that we would go to
Costa Rica next so now it was time to
look back on the past month of
travel making our way in right now
[Music]
boys even when you live it to the
fullest life is short we get placed on
this earth unwillingly live our lives
and then we die and one of the biggest
factors that held me back from achieving
my dreams and traveling the world was
fear I had fear about making new friends
I had fear about where I would go with
little sense of direction I had fears
about money my future about if this was
the right decision or if it was a big
waste of
time it's in these moments where I take
a step back and try to see that fear
lives in the future and you need to stay
present taking a big jump and learning
to grow wings as you fall has completely
taught me how to fly it taught me how to
really live and it's opened new doors as
to what I now know is
possible now I'm not saying to go spend
all your money on a flight and go broke
traveling the world seeking wisdom to
find your inner self but I am here to
tell you that it's not the 1900s anymore
it's the age of the internet and you can
turn almost any passion of yours into a
career these days so think big don't
settle for less at least not before
taking the
[Music]
risk
[Music]
wait Josh step back for one second [ __ ]
you my wrists are sweaty [ __ ] I told
you to break that [ __ ] and then make
that [ __ ] noce got [ __ ] super glue
put it on you would never notice may not
testing that [ __ ] just got [ __ ] super
up my [ __ ] [ __ ] the
head [ __ ]
